zh.lifeSummary: Sir Thurston Le Boteler是Thurstan de Bastenburg和Lady Juliane Beaudesert De Murdac的兒子，於1009年出生在法國諾門迪曼其拉 海也-度-普易次。他和Anna de la Haye至少有1個兒子。他去世於1074年7月4日在法國巴-諾門迪曼其庫塔恩斯享年65歲，埋葬在法國巴-諾門迪曼其庫塔恩斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: Sir Thurston Le Boteler是Thurstan de Bastenburg和Lady Juliane Beaudesert De Murdac的儿子，于1009年出生在法国諾門迪曼其拉 海也-度-普易次。他和Anna de la Haye至少有1个儿子。他去世于1074年7月4日在法国巴-諾門迪曼其庫塔恩斯享年65岁，埋葬在法国巴-諾門迪曼其庫塔恩斯。

nameMeanings.name: Le
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Vietnamese Thanh, Hung, Minh, Dung, Tuan, Hai, Hoa, Thuy, Son, Anh, Long, Hoang.  Chinese Hong, Sang, Chi, Dong, Tong, Lai, Hang, Man, Han, Ho, Phoung, Chan.  Korean Tam, Phong, Nam, Thai, Tuong, Chung, Thach, Tinh, Manh, Tham, Uyen, Vang.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 樂, meaning ‘happiness’ in Chinese: from the first element of the personal name Le Fu (樂父), style name of Yan, son of the Duke Dai of Song (ruler of the state of Song, reigned 799–766  BC ). Yan's grandson Le Ju (樂莒) inherited Le (樂) as his surname.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 勒: (i) from the second element of the placename Shu-Le (疏勒), an ancient state (located in present-day Xinjiang Uyghur autonomous region) during the Western Han dynasty (206  BC –25  AD ). (ii) a surname near the Bohai Sea region during the Han dynasty (206  BC –220  AD ). (iii) a surname traced back to the Hun ethnic group in northern China during the Jin dynasty (265–420  AD ).

variants.name: Yue
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese: Chi, Feng, Gang, Wei, Chun, Guo, Jin, Kin, Ping, Yan, Yun, Zhang, Chau, Du, Hau, Hung, My, Sau.

Mandarin form of the surname 岳 (or 嶽), meaning ‘high mountain’ in Chinese: traced back to Si Yue (四岳 or 四嶽), said to be the title of an official post in charge of sacrificial rituals on the site of famous mountains during the reign of the legendary Emperor Yao (c. 24th century BC ) and Emperor Shun (c. 23rd century BC ).

variants.name: Lok
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Ping, Tat, Wing, Ying Lin, Chi Ming, Ching Wah, Chun, Chung Man, Han, Kam, Kam Tim, Kin.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surname 樂, see   Le   1.
Chinese: Cantonese form of the surnames 陸, 鹿, 逯 and 禄, see   Lu   5 and (7-9).

variants.name: Li
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Chinese Wei, Ming, Li, Jian, Hong, Yan, Ping, Jing, Yong, Ying, Hui, Jin, Min, Chung.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 李, meaning ‘plum’: (i) said to have been adopted in place of the Chinese surname 理 (also pronounced Li; meaning ‘rules’ in Chinese) by Li Zhen (利貞), son of an official called Li Zheng (理徵) in the late Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ). It is said that Li Zhen's father incurred the displeasure of King Zhou of Shang (died 1046  BC ) and Li Zhen fled to a wasteland area, where he survived on wild plums. After his return to civilization, he changed his surname to Li (李 ‘plum’), a word with the same pronunciation. The original surname, 理, is said to be from Li Guan (理官), the name of a post of officials in charge of legislation in ancient China. Li Er (李耳), also known as Lao Tzu or Lao Zi, traditionally regarded as the founder of Taoism, is believed to have been a descendant of Li Zhen. (ii) adopted as a surname by the Chi Li (叱李) family from the Xianbei ethnic group during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ). (iii) in the early years of the Tang dynasty (618–907  AD ), sixteen high officials with different surnames were granted the surname Li (李), the surname of the royal family of the Tang dynasty, by Emperor Taizong of Tang (died 649  BC ), because of their contributions to the establishment of the Tang regime. (iv) the surname can also be traced back to several minority ethnic groups in ancient China.

Chinese: Mandarin form of the surname 黎, meaning ‘black’ or ‘multitude’ in ancient Chinese: (i) from Li (黎), the name of an ancient state (located in Changzhi in Shanxi province) during the Shang dynasty (1600–1046  BC ), which was annexed in the Western Zhou dynasty (1046–771  BC ) and later granted to Fengshu, also known as Li Hou (黎侯, 'Marquis Li'). (ii) adopted as a surname by the Su Li (素黎) family, members of the Xianbei ethnic group, during the Northern Wei dynasty (386–534  AD ).

nameMeanings.name: Boteler
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English: occupational name for a maker of leather bottles, from an agent derivative of Middle English botel, Old French bouteille ‘bottle’.

nameMeanings.name: Thurston
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: from the Anglo-Norman, Anglo-Scandinavian, and Middle English personal name Thurstan, Thorsten (Old Norse Thórsteinn, Thorsten, Anglicized as Old English Thurstān; see Thorstensen ).
habitational name from Thurston in Suffolk, so called from the genitive case of the Old Norse personal name Thóri (see Thor ) + Old English tūn ‘enclosure, settlement’.

variants.name: Tustin
meanings: Transferred use of the surname, which is of uncertain origin, probably a Norman form of the Old Norse personal name Þórsteinn, composed of elements meaning ‘Thor's stone’. It is now used fairly regularly as a given name, largely as a result of the fame of the film actor Dustin Hoffman ( b. 1937 ), who is said to have been named in honour of the less well-known silent film actor Dustin Farman ( 1870–1929 ).

variants.name: Thorstensen
meanings: Norwegian and Danish: patronymic from the personal name Thorsten, a variant of Thorstein, Old Norse Thórsteinn, composed of the name of the god Thórr (see Thor ) + steinn ‘stone, rock’, hence meaning ‘altar of Thor’ or perhaps ‘hammer of Thor’.

en.lifeSummary: Sir Thurston Le Boteler was born in 1009, in La Haye-du-Puits, Manche, Normandy, France as the son of Thurstan de Bastenburg and Lady Juliane Beaudesert De Murdac. He had at least 1 son with Anna de la Haye. He died on 4 July 1074, in Coutances, Manche, Lower Normandy, France, at the age of 65, and was buried in Coutances, Manche, Lower Normandy, France.

fr.lifeSummary: Sir Thurston Le Boteler est né en 1009, à La Haye-du-Puits, Manche, Normandie, France, fils de Thurstan de Bastenburg et Lady Juliane Beaudesert De Murdac. Il a eu au moins 1 fils avec Anna de la Haye. Il est décédé le 4 juillet 1074, à Coutances, Manche, Basse-Normandie, France, à l’âge de 65 ans, et a été enterré à Coutances, Manche, Basse-Normandie, France.

de.lifeSummary: Sir Thurston Le Boteler wurde 1009 in La Haye-du-Puits, Manche, Normandie, Frankreich, als Sohn von Thurstan de Bastenburg und Lady Juliane Beaudesert De Murdac geboren. Er hatte mit Anna de la Haye mindestens 1 Sohn. Er starb am 4. Juli 1074 im Alter von 65 Jahren in Coutances, Manche, Basse-Normandie, Frankreich und wurde in Coutances, Manche, Basse-Normandie, Frankreich begraben.

it.lifeSummary: Sir Thurston Le Boteler è nato il nel 1009 nel La Haye-du-Puits, Manche, Normandie, Francia come figlio di Thurstan de Bastenburg e Lady Juliane Beaudesert De Murdac. Lui avuto almeno 1 figlio con Anna de la Haye. Morì il 4 luglio 1074, nel Coutances, Manche, Basse-Normandie, Francia, all'età di 65 anni, e fu sepolto a Coutances, Manche, Basse-Normandie, Francia.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Thurston Le Botelerは1009年にフランスノーマンディマンチェラ ヘイエ-ドウ-プウイツでThurstan de BastenburgとLady Juliane Beaudesert De Murdacの息子として生まれた。彼はAnna de la Hayeとの間に少なくとも1人の息子をもうけた。彼は1074年7月4日にフランスバセ-ノーマンディマンチェコウタンセスで65歳で死去し，フランスバセ-ノーマンディマンチェコウタンセスにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Thurston Le Boteler(은)는 1009년 프랑스 노먼디 먼체 라 헤예-드-푸이츠에서 Thurstan de Bastenburg(와)과 Lady Juliane Beaudesert De Murdac의 아들로 태어났다. 그는 Anna de la Haye(와)과 적어도 한 명의 아들을 두었다. 그는 1074년 7월 4일 프랑스 배세-노먼디 먼체 코우탠스스에서 65세의 나이로 사망했으며 프랑스 배세-노먼디 먼체 코우탠스스에 묻혔다.

pt.lifeSummary: Sir Thurston Le Boteler nasceu em 1009, em La Haye-du-Puits, Manche, Normandie, França filho de Thurstan de Bastenburg e Lady Juliane Beaudesert De Murdac. Ele teve pelo menos 1 filho com Anna de la Haye. Ele faleceu em 4 de julho de 1074, em Coutances, Manche, Basse-Normandie, França, com 65 anos, e foi sepultado em Coutances, Manche, Basse-Normandie, França.

ru.lifeSummary: Sir Thurston Le Boteler родился в 1009 г. в местности ла хеие-ду-пуиц, манч, норманди, Франция. Его родителей звали Thurstan de Bastenburg и Lady Juliane Beaudesert De Murdac. У него был по крайней мере 1 сын от Anna de la Haye. Он умер 4 июля 1074 г. в местности коутансесь, манч, бассе-норманди, Франция в возрасте 65 лет и был похоронен в местности коутансесь, манч, бассе-норманди, Франция.

es.lifeSummary: Sir Thurston Le Boteler nació en 1009, en La Haye-du-Puits, Manche, Normandie, Francia como hijo de Thurstan de Bastenburg y Lady Juliane Beaudesert De Murdac. Tuvo por lo menos 1 hijo con Anna de la Haye. Murió el 4 de julio de 1074, en Coutances, Manche, Basse-Normandie, Francia, a la edad de 65 años, y fue sepultado en Coutances, Manche, Basse-Normandie, Francia.
